description:
The named service is provided by the unbound package. The unbound package can be removed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf remove unbound
rationale:
If there is no need to make DNS server software available,
removing it provides a safeguard against its activation.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must not have the unbound package installed.
stig.fixtext:
To remove the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 package unbound run the following command:

$ sudo dnf remove unbound
stig.checktext:
Verify that Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 does not have a Domain Name Server (DNS) server package installed with the following command:

$ dnf list --installed unbound

package unbound is not installed

If the "unbound" package is installed and the information system security officer (ISSO) lacks a documented requirement for a DNS server, this is a finding.
stig.vuldiscussion: If system is not a Domain Name Server (DNS) server it shouldn't have a DNS server package installed to decrease the attack surface of the system.

title: Uninstall unbound Package
